decisions.title: Prioritize Sift Edge v0.1.0 Release Over Core Platform Stability
decisions.context: Edge sync prototype (ENG-8855) and v0.1.0 release (ENG-9569) are progressing while critical performance issues remain (Chrome crashes ENG-9138, chart zoom errors ENG-8866). This tests 'Build the Moat' vs 'Nail the Core' priority.

arguments.for: Edge capabilities differentiate us competitively and enable offline decision capture (North Star metric). Early release builds moat before competitors.
arguments.against: Core platform instability (crashes, errors) directly blocks current customers from capturing decisions. Nail the Core principle demands we fix what exists before shipping new surfaces.

decisions.title: Approve Data-Batcher Rust Rewrite Resource Allocation
decisions.context: Team forking data-batcher codebase (ENG-9566, ENG-9565, ENG-9567) while Kafka message contracts still in design phase (ENG-9270, ENG-9425). Significant engineering investment in infrastructure vs. user-facing features.
decisions.blockedBy: Kafka message schema not finalized (ENG-9270). Cannot validate architectural decisions without contract definition.

arguments.for: Rust rewrite will improve data pipeline performance, enabling higher-rate telemetry capture (supports North Star). Infrastructure investment necessary for Canvas scale.
arguments.against: Premature optimization. Schema design incomplete. Resources could address 8 customer-facing bugs blocking decision capture today. Evidence of need unclear.

decisions.title: Consolidate Feature Flags Before Canvas GA Launch
decisions.context: Rules/stats feature flags need consolidation (ENG-9557) while Canvas features ship with Family Data still in Beta (ENG-9556). Flag sprawl creates technical debt and testing complexity.

arguments.for: Clean feature flag architecture reduces testing surface, prevents configuration errors, and enables faster iteration on Canvas core features (Nail the Core).
arguments.against: Flag consolidation is non-user-facing work. Delaying Canvas features to clean up flags may slow market momentum and decision capture adoption.

decisions.title: Defer E2E External Integration Testing (Checkly) Implementation
decisions.context: Evaluating Checkly for external testing (ENG-9585) while ephemeral environments auto-destroy on PR close (ENG-9390) is incomplete. Testing infrastructure expanding before dev workflow stabilizes.

arguments.for: External integration testing catches production-environment issues earlier, preventing customer-facing incidents that block decision capture. Evidence: sharelink behavior bugs (ENG-9399, ENG-9563).
arguments.against: Internal dev workflow (ephemeral envs) incomplete. Signal>Evidence>Decision: Do we have evidence that current testing gaps cause customer impact? Focus should be customer-facing bug fixes first.

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy coordination week: Kafka schema reviews, Edge release planning, performance war rooms. 30 Linear issues triaged across Core/Edge/Canvas.
alerts: Critical: Only 2 focus blocks this week for decision-making. Strategy work requires 4x2hr blocks minimum.
Performance issues (ENG-9138, ENG-8866) unaddressed for 12+ days. Escalate or re-prioritize.
3 architectural decisions blocked on Kafka schema (ENG-9270). Schedule design finalization session.
8 customer-facing bugs in backlog. Tactical sprint needed before Edge v0.1.0 launch.
Zero time allocated to North Star metric analysis. How do we measure Decisions Captured without instrumentation?
